key_pointers.benchmark_func: engine/catalog/catalog_bench_test.go: BenchmarkStripedVsGlobalLock (top-level, with b.Run("Striped", ...) and b.Run("GlobalLock", ...) sub-benchmarks)
key_pointers.harness_types: engine/catalog/catalog_bench_test.go: stripedRecordStore (256-shard, per-stripe sync.Mutex, reuses production stripeFor(fileID)) and globalLockRecordStore (single sync.Mutex over one shared map) -- both benchmark-only, never referenced by production code
key_pointers.prior_subtask: task-2a.3.1 stress test: engine/catalog/catalog_test.go TestStripedConcurrencyStress (commit 15f5dd5, verified)
key_pointers.production_reference: engine/catalog/catalog.go: Catalog struct, stripeFor(fileID), stripes[numStripes]sync.Mutex, indexMu, activeMu -- the real design this benchmark is modeled after

measured_numbers.ratio: Striped ~1.97x faster (lower ns/op) than GlobalLock, reproduced across -benchtime=200ms/300ms/500ms/default(1s) runs

design_deviation_note: First implementation attempt wrapped the real disk-backed Catalog + FileManager for BOTH variants (global-lock baseline as a stripe-free variant of the same disk-backed CRUD path). This measured no reliable direction (both ~2000-2800 ns/op, noise-dominated) because pread/pwrite syscall cost (~1-2us) swamped the sub-100ns lock-contention signal. Per the subtask's own explicit latitude ('does not need to be a full production-quality alternate implementation, just a benchmarking harness faithful enough'), pivoted to a minimal in-memory harness (stripedRecordStore/globalLockRecordStore) that reuses Catalog's real stripeFor(fileID) hash and CatalogRecord.Encode/Decode but stores bytes in-memory, isolating lock granularity as the only variable. This is documented in-line in catalog_bench_test.go's harness doc comment.
validation_matrix_status: all 4 criteria satisfied (see validation-matrix.json): benchmark-exists, no-regression (go test ./engine/catalog/... -race -v -count=1 PASS), build-clean (gofmt/go build/go vet clean), direction-demonstrated (~2x, verified via manual runs)

closes.parent_task: task-2a.3 (both 2a.3.1 and 2a.3.2 now implemented; 2a.3.1 already verified, 2a.3.2 pending /cdr:verify)
closes.github_issue: #8 -- becomes closable once this subtask is verified
